stemHtml: Why may the artificial horizon indicate a climb during acceleration on the take-off roll?

options.a: The aircraft symbol’s position on the instrument is only correct in straight and level flight and it is displaced during accelerations.
options.b: The mercury in the longitudinally mounted switch will hang back and complete the circuit to the pitch torque motor, causing the instrument to show a false climb.
options.c: This statement is not true. Artificial horizons do not suffer from acceleration errors.
options.d: The artificial horizon is only calibrated for straight, level, accelerated flight.
